title: The association between in utero exposure to maternal psychological stress and female reproductive function in adolescence: A prospective cohort study.
ehr_evidence: None; the study uses a population-based pregnancy cohort (The Raine Study) with self-reported questionnaires and clinical measurements.
summary: A prospective cohort study investigating whether maternal psychological stress (stressful life events) during pregnancy affects reproductive function in female offspring during adolescence.
key_findings: Maternal stressful life events in late gestation were associated with greater uterine volume and higher ovarian antral follicle count (AFC) in adolescent offspring.
No associations were found between maternal stress and circulating AMH or Inhibin B.
